title: Startup Website Launch
context: A fintech startup is launching their MVP website. They've sent over a requirements doc but the PM has clearly been talking to too many stakeholders.

requirements.text: Landing page must explain the product clearly in under 30 seconds
requirements.explanation: Clarity and speed of understanding is essential for a landing page — users decide to stay or leave within seconds.

requirements.text: Include comprehensive detailed documentation of all features
requirements.explanation: Detailed docs are important but live on separate pages — not the landing page where brevity is key.

requirements.text: The design should feel trustworthy and professional
requirements.explanation: Trust is critical for fintech — users will not enter financial details on a site that feels sketchy.

requirements.text: Use bold, disruptive design — break conventions to stand out
requirements.explanation: "Trustworthy and professional" directly conflicts with "break conventions" — especially for a financial product.

requirements.text: Include a sign-up form above the fold
requirements.explanation: The primary conversion action must be visible immediately — this is the whole point of a launch page.

requirements.text: No sign-up wall — let people explore freely first
requirements.explanation: Requiring sign-up above the fold and having no sign-up wall are directly contradictory conversion strategies.

requirements.text: Add social proof: testimonials or logos of early users
requirements.explanation: Social proof significantly increases conversion but requires existing customer relationships.

requirements.text: Make the page accessible for screen readers (WCAG AA)
requirements.explanation: Accessibility is a legal requirement in many markets and a moral imperative.

teachingMoment: Conversion strategy contradictions ("sign up immediately" vs "no sign-up wall") are common when multiple stakeholders add requirements without coordinating.
